claims.claim: The held-out contamination report passes n-gram, embedding-similarity (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2), and time-shift checks.
Path B training data contains 158 preference pairs (62 wrong tasks x up to 3 chosen, train-only, cross-dimension fallback).
The embedded candidate-output baseline passes 62.16% of held-out tasks.
The embedded candidate-output baseline passes 67.65% of dev tasks.
The Week 10 generation baseline scores 74.59% average weighted score on the held-out partition.
The prompt-engineered base model (untrained Qwen3.5-0.8B + explicit policy prompt) scores 50.07% average weighted score on held-out.
The ORPO-trained adapter scores 72.25% average weighted score on the held-out partition.
Delta A: ORPO-trained adapter vs Week 10 baseline on held-out = -2.34 percentage points (not statistically significant).
claims.notes: p-value=0.7095, 95% CI [-11.09, +6.20]. Not significant — adapter nearly matches but does not clearly surpass Week 10 baseline.

claims.claim: Delta B: ORPO-trained adapter vs prompt-engineered base model on held-out = +22.18 percentage points (highly significant, p=0.0, 95% CI [+14.43, +29.82]).
claims.notes: p-value=0.0, 95% CI [+14.43, +29.82]. Entire CI is positive — training benefit is unambiguous. Training instilled Tenacious policy knowledge far beyond what prompting alone achieves.

claims.claim: Delta C: Trained adapter binary pass rate vs Week 10 baseline binary pass rate = 0.00 pts (both 23/37, 62.16%). Not significant.
claims.notes: p-value=0.5495, 95% CI [-24.32, +24.32]. Both conditions pass 23/37 held-out tasks at threshold 0.70. Average weighted scores differ (72.25% vs 74.59%) but threshold-crossing rate is unchanged at ~200 training steps.

claims.claim: Signal grounding dimension improved from 0.718 to 0.836 post-training (+11.8 pts).
ORPO training: Qwen3.5-0.8B, LoRA r=16 alpha=32, beta=0.2, lr=2e-5, early stopped ~200 steps, 158 pairs.
